title: Noun gender
subtitle: Masculine, feminine, and neuter — the rules and exceptions

hook.text: Every Russian noun has a gender — masculine, feminine, or neuter. This isn't biological sex; it's a grammatical category that affects every adjective, pronoun, and verb that agrees with the noun. You can predict gender from the ending most of the time. Today you learn the rules — and the exceptions that trip everyone up.

why.text: Gender is the foundation of Russian agreement. If you get the gender wrong, the adjective ending is wrong, the pronoun is wrong, and the verb agreement is wrong. Everything downstream of gender becomes an error. Getting gender right from the start is more important than memorising complex grammar — it's the root of correctness.

cards.title: Gender from the ending — the core rules
cards.body: Most noun gender is predictable from the final letter. Three rules cover about 90% of nouns.

cards.title: Nouns ending in Ь — the split
cards.body: Nouns ending in Ь (soft sign) can be EITHER masculine OR feminine. There is no rule — you must learn the gender with the word. This is the biggest source of gender errors for learners.

cards.title: Exceptions and invariable nouns
cards.body: Some nouns defy the ending rules. People words follow biological gender regardless of ending (мужчина is masculine). Borrowed words that end in vowels other than А/О/Е don't decline and are usually neuter.

examples.meaning: cafe — neuter, ends in Е, doesn't decline
taxi — neuter, ends in И, doesn't decline
name — neuter, ends in Я (special declension)

questions.explanation: книга ends in А — feminine. The rule is reliable here.

questions.prompt: What gender are nouns ending in Ь?
options: Either masculine or feminine — must be learned with the word
questions.answer: Either masculine or feminine — must be learned with the word
questions.explanation: Ь-ending nouns split between masculine and feminine. No rule — learn gender with the word.

cards.title: Why gender matters — agreement
cards.body: Every adjective modifying a noun must agree in gender, number, and case. The word 'my' has four forms: мой (masculine), моя (feminine), моё (neuter), мои (plural). Get the gender wrong and the possessive is wrong. The same applies to красивый/красивая/красивое (beautiful), новый/новая/новое (new), etc.

cards.title: Learning gender as part of the word
cards.body: The most effective strategy: never learn a noun alone. Always learn it with a determiner that shows its gender. Learn мой дом (not just дом), моя книга (not just книга). This drills the agreement pattern into muscle memory alongside the word. The mnemonic: мой = masculine, моя = feminine, моё = neuter.

examples.meaning: my (m): мой дом, мой год, мой словарь
my (f): моя книга, моя школа, моя дверь
my (n): моё слово, моё море, моё кафе

questions.explanation: стол ends in a consonant — masculine.
дверь ends in Ь — this must be learned. It is feminine.

questions.prompt: What is the correct form of 'my' for словарь (dictionary, masculine)?
questions.explanation: мой is the masculine form. словарь is masculine, so: мой словарь.
кафе is a borrowed word ending in Е — neuter. It doesn't decline.

rules: А / Я ending → feminine (книга, школа, земля)
О / Е ending → neuter (слово, море, окно)
Ь ending → EITHER masculine OR feminine — learn with the word
Borrowed words ending in non-standard vowels → usually neuter, don't decline
People words follow biological gender regardless of ending
summary.nextLesson: Next: plural forms — how nouns change when there's more than one.

questions.prompt: What is 'my' for a feminine noun?
кафе doesn't decline. What gender is it?
